title: Warm Roasted Algerian Butternut Squash Tagine
description: A funky and creamy Algerian dish featuring roasted butternut squash, perfect for a low-fat meal prep option. This tagine is a hearty and flavorful stew made with tender butternut squash and a blend of aromatic spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the butternut squash with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until the squash is evenly coated.
Spread the squash out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 30 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
In a large pot, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Stir in the ground cumin and smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute.
Add the roasted butternut squash, chickpeas, and chicken broth to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together, about 20 minutes.
Stir in the plain yogurt and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
